note: Development run on RR-9 fold only (MPS, 5 epochs). Full LOMO requires HPC.
method: Geneformer fine-tuning (ENSMUSG→ENSG ortholog mapping, rank tokenization, BertForSequenceClassification)

comparison.verdict: Geneformer (dev) substantially underperforms classical ML on small-n (47 train) — expected, consistent with literature

limitations: Ortholog coverage ~57%: 42% of genes lost in ENSMUSG->ENSG mapping
Train avg seq len 1593 vs test avg 618 (RR-9 sparsity)
Geneformer pretrained on human scRNA, not mouse bulk
next_steps: Run full 4-fold LOMO on Cayuga HPC (sbatch hpc_submit_geneformer.sh)
Use 10 epochs, batch_size=16 on A40 GPU
Consider layer freezing (only fine-tune classifier head) if overfitting persists
